category_title: Non-verbal students with SID using pre-symbolic communication (gestures, sounds, eye contact)
minimal_context: The student is participating in a daily play routine in a natural school setting.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for our daily play routine.

STUDENT: (Approaches the play area, makes eye contact, and uses sounds to communicate.)

TUTOR: Here is the puzzle, I already took all the pieces out for you.

STUDENT: (Uses facial expressions and gestures, reaching a hand out toward the tutor.)

TUTOR: Here is the first piece, I will put it in the right spot for you.

STUDENT: (Signs her wants by withdrawal, breaking eye contact and demonstrating a communication arrest.)

options.A: Grant full agency by telling the student, 'I don't care, just pick out a puzzle piece you like.'
options.B: Put her in a position where she is less distracted, so she is not seeing other things.
options.C: Provide information regarding the nature of the communication breakdown during the play routine.
options.D: Ignore her and move on to the next activity in the daily routine.
